description: Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting

description: Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting

description: Symptom: In a VXLAN environment, AOS-CXswitches may fails to install routes in the routing table, causing traffic loss for certain destinations. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing process using the following: Copy switch# start-shell
